dismissalText.commentary: Sanju Samson b Burger 6 (7b 1x4 0x6 11m) SR: 85.71

dismissalComment.html: bowled him!! The off stump is flattened. Burger fires one at 140kph, and Samson is beaten after looking to heave across the line. On a length on middle, and the ball seams away after pitching. Samson went swinging without any foot movement, and missed. His first innings in yellow, against the side he made a name with, is cut short early

images.caption: Nandre Burger got Sanju Samson with one that nipped away
images.longCaption: Nandre Burger got Sanju Samson with one that nipped away, Rajasthan Royals vs Chennai Super Kings, IPL, Guwahati, March 30, 2026

dismissalText.commentary: Ruturaj Gaikwad b Archer 6 (11b 1x4 0x6 17m) SR: 54.54

dismissalComment.html: gone!! Archer wins the battle against an off-colour Gaikwad. Again the CSK captain looked to make room and attack, but this time failed to get bat on ball. Archer kept the ball fullish and straight, and Gaikwad, looking to open the face of the bat and perhaps slice it, missed. The stumps lit up. There was the swag on Archer's face after launching that ball at 143kph

dismissalText.commentary: Ayush Mhatre c †Jurel b Burger 0 (1b 0x4 0x6 7m) SR: 0

dismissalComment.html: loud appeal for caught behind - and with extreme confidence from Burger and Jurel - but it's turned down. RR review, though. It was a bouncer from Burger that left Mhatre nearly cramped. The ball landed on leg, and took off to pass close to Mhatre's head. He looked to pull that late, but was beaten by the pace and the bounce. Ohh, there's a spike on UltraEdge as the ball zips past the glove. It brushes his bottom thumb, and Mhatre has to go. A first-ball duck for Mhatre, and CSK have lost three wickets already. In fact, Burger is on a hat-trick

dismissalText.commentary: Matthew Short c Jaiswal b Sandeep Sharma 2 (7b 0x4 0x6 18m) SR: 28.57

dismissalComment.html: chipped to midwicket!! Jaiswal, who took the catch, almost gestures that he couldn't spot the ball. The ball stopped on the pitch before coming on to Short, who ended up pushing at it aerially off a fullish length on the stumps. Or maybe Sandeep with a lack of pace. Either way, Short falls as CSK lose their fourth within the powerplay

dismissalText.commentary: Sarfaraz Khan lbw b Jadeja 17 (12b 2x4 1x6 21m) SR: 141.66

dismissalComment.html: Jurel and Jadeja are delighted while appealing, and Sarfaraz is given lbw. But he reviews. Sarfaraz went sweeping, but missed, as the ball went beneath his bat. The first replay hints that it could be too high as he's hit on his back, but we'll see. No bat involved is the confirmation from the third umpire. Ohh, in fact, the ball pitches on middle, and straightens enough for Ball Tracking to project it to hit the top of middle. Jadeja strikes in his first over

dismissalText.commentary: Kartik Sharma lbw b Brijesh Sharma 18 (15b 0x4 1x6 24m) SR: 120

dismissalComment.html: That should be out, finger goes up. Kartik chats... and takes the review. 134ks full ball on middle and leg. Kartik's forward stride was small and he tried to drive it through mid-on. Bat well in front of body, beat the inside-edge and hit the outer-half of the front pad. On the shin. Ball-tracking shows it's hitting leg stump and Brijesh has his first IPL wicket.

dismissalText.commentary: Shivam Dube c Ravi Bishnoi b Jadeja 6 (4b 0x4 1x6 2m) SR: 150

dismissalComment.html: number six!! Jadeja's experience trumps Dube's aggression. The RR spinner goes full and wider of off, tempting Dube to launch him down the ground again. Dube takes the bait, but ends up finding long-off. It was a bit of a cat and mouse between the two right from the first ball, and Jadeja won't mind taking the wicket after being hit for six first ball

dismissalText.commentary: Jamie Overton run out (Hetmyer/†Jurel) 43 (36b 2x4 2x6 61m) SR: 119.44

dismissalComment.html: Has a hoick, doesn't time it. Rolls to deep midwicket, Hetmyer throws it casually to the keeper. Overton ambled the first, as he finished the run he realised he can nick a second. But the throw, on the bounce, came to the keeper at a good height. Off went the bails and he ran through towards the pavillion

dismissalText.commentary: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi c Khan b Kamboj 52 (17b 4x4 5x6 31m) SR: 305.88

dismissalComment.html: taken at deep extra cover!! Sarfaraz takes a catch while running in and diving forward. Sooryavanshi nearly refuses to leave the field. He eventually goes back disappointed. Kamboj angled this ball across at the left-hander, and bowled at a length. The ball came to Sooryavanshi outside off, and he went lofting through the line. That was hit hard and flat, but went right into Sarfaraz's hands
